query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "dvao: dynamic variance-adaptive advantage optimization for multi-rewar" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) as it proposes a reinforcement learning framework (MERL) that explicitly utilizes a variance-based metric ($\mathcal{V}^{ex}$, fraction of variance explained) to optimize policy gradients, which directly addresses the "Dynamic Variance-adaptive" mechanism and "Multi-reward" optimization context of the user's question, even though the specific application domain (general RL benchmarks) differs from the user's "linguistics" focus.
